briefdescription.para: Vector representing the negative X axis.
Vector representing the negative Y axis.
Vector representing the negative Z axis.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
x
(or width) component
y
(or height) component
z
(or depth) component

rhs
The vector to test against
True if the vectors are not equal

Asserts if index is out of range. Should be 0, 1 or 2. @SINCE_1_0.0
index
Subscript index
The float at the given index

Asserts if index is out of range. Should be 0, 1 or 2. @SINCE_1_0.0
index
Subscript index
The float at the given index

briefdescription.para: Returns the dot product of this vector and another vector.

detaileddescription.para:
The dot product is the length of one vector in the direction of another vector. This is great for lighting, threshold testing the angle between two unit vectors, calculating the distance between two points in a particular direction. @SINCE_1_0.0
other
The other vector
The dot product

briefdescription.para: Returns the cross produce of this vector and another vector.

detaileddescription.para: The cross produce of two vectors is a vector which is perpendicular to the plane of the two vectors. This is great for calculating normals and making matrices orthogonal.

@SINCE_1_0.0
other
The other vector
The cross product

briefdescription.para: Returns the length of the vector.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The length of the vector

briefdescription.para: Returns the length of the vector squared.

detaileddescription.para:
This is more efficient than Length() for threshold testing as it avoids the use of a square root. @SINCE_1_0.0
The length of the vector squared

briefdescription.para: Sets the vector to be unit length, whilst maintaining its direction.

detaileddescription.para: @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Clamps the vector between minimum and maximum vectors.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
min
The minimum vector
max
The maximum vector

briefdescription.para: Returns the contents of the vector as an array of 3 floats.

detaileddescription.para:
The order of the values in this array are as follows: 0: x (or width, or r) 1: y (or height, or g) 2: z (or depth, or b) @SINCE_1_0.0
The vector contents as an array of 3 floats
inlined for performance reasons (generates less code than a function call)

briefdescription.para: Returns the contents of the vector as an array of 3 floats.

detaileddescription.para:
The order of the values in this array are as follows: 0: x (or width, or r) 1: y (or height, or g) 2: z (or depth, or b) @SINCE_1_0.0
The vector contents as an array of 3 floats
inlined for performance reasons (generates less code than a function call)

briefdescription.para: Returns the x & y components (or width & height, or r & g) as a Vector2.
Returns the x & y components (or width & height, or r & g) as a Vector2.
Returns the y & z components (or height & depth, or g & b) as a Vector2.
Returns the y & z components (or height & depth, or g & b) as a Vector2.
